description: Restructure fin CLI into an agent-native toolbelt with JSON envelope contracts, tool registry, health checks, and /fin Claude Code skill

userStories.title: Create JSON envelope module and tool registry
userStories.description: As a CLI developer, I need the envelope.ts and tool.ts infrastructure modules so that all commands can output structured JSON envelopes and self-register in a tool catalog
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at packages/cli/src/envelope.ts exporting ok(), fail(), emitRaw(), isJsonMode(), rethrowCapture(), setCaptureMode(), setJsonModeOverride(), EnvelopeCapture
BLOCKING_CODES set contains NO_CONFIG, NO_DATABASE, SCHEMA_MISMATCH
ok() writes exactly one JSON line to stdout and calls process.exit(0)
fail() writes error envelope and exits with code 1 or 2 for blocking codes
File exists at packages/cli/src/tool.ts exporting defineToolCommand(), toolRegistry[], ToolMeta, ParameterMeta types
defineToolCommand() extracts parameters from citty args and pushes metadata to toolRegistry[]
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Copy patterns from .tmp/let/packages/cli/src/envelope.ts and .tmp/let/packages/cli/src/tool.ts. Adapt BLOCKING_CODES for fin domain (NO_CONFIG, NO_DATABASE, SCHEMA_MISMATCH instead of NO_CONFIG, NO_SOURCES, SCHEMA_MISMATCH)

userStories.title: Split entry point into index.ts and main.ts
userStories.description: As a CLI developer, I need index.ts to be a thin executable wrapper and main.ts to be an importable command tree so that tests can import commands without side effects
acceptanceCriteria: File packages/cli/src/main.ts exports a main command via defineCommand() with all subcommands
File packages/cli/src/index.ts imports main from ./main, calls initConfig() in setup, and runs runMain(main)
Existing CLI commands still work: bun run fin view accounts, bun run fin report cashflow --group=personal
main.ts has no process.exit() or runMain() calls
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Reference .tmp/let/packages/cli/src/index.ts and .tmp/let/packages/cli/src/main.ts. Keep existing subcommands wired in main.ts alongside new ones as they are added

userStories.title: Implement tools command
userStories.description: As an agent, I need fin tools --json to return the full tool catalog so I can discover available commands and their parameters
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/tools/index.ts exporting toolsCommand
fin tools --json outputs a valid JSON envelope with data.tools array and data.total count
fin tools (text mode) prints tool catalog grouped by category to stderr
fin tools <name> --json returns single tool detail or NOT_FOUND error
toolsCommand uses plain defineCommand() (not defineToolCommand -- infrastructure, not a tool)
Command is wired in main.ts subCommands
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Reference .tmp/let/packages/cli/src/commands/tools/index.ts. Global flags for fin: --json, --db, --format

userStories.title: Implement health command
userStories.description: As an agent, I need fin health --json to check prerequisites (config, DB, rules, inbox) and report status with actionable fix commands
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/health/index.ts exporting healthCommand

Checks: config file exists (data/fin.config.toml, blocking), config validates (blocking), database exists (data/fin.db, info), rules file exists (data/fin.rules.ts, degraded), inbox directory exists (imports/inbox/, info)
fin health --json outputs envelope with data.status (ready|degraded|blocked), data.checks array, data.summary
Each check has id, label, status, severity, detail, fix fields
Exit code 2 when status is blocked, 0 otherwise
Text mode prints human-readable check list
Command is wired in main.ts subCommands
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Reference .tmp/let/packages/cli/src/commands/health/index.ts. Adapt checks for fin: config TOML, SQLite DB, rules TS, inbox dir. Use emitRaw() for health envelope (builds its own)

userStories.title: Implement config show and config validate commands
userStories.description: As an agent, I need config show --json and config validate --json to inspect and verify the configuration before querying data
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/config/index.ts exporting configCommand group
File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/config/show.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'config.show'
File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/config/validate.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'config.validate'
config show --json returns envelope with data.groups, data.accounts, data.financial, data.configPath
config validate --json returns envelope with data.valid boolean and data.errors array
Both commands appear in fin tools --json catalog
Text mode outputs readable config display
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Use getConfig(), getGroupIds(), getGroupMetadata(), getAccountsByGroup() from @fin/core. Config commands catch NO_CONFIG and INVALID_CONFIG errors

userStories.title: Implement view accounts and view transactions commands
userStories.description: As an agent, I need view accounts --json and view transactions --json to query account balances and transaction lists with filters
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/view/index.ts exporting viewCommand group
File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/view/accounts.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'view.accounts'
File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/view/transactions.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'view.transactions'
view accounts --json returns envelope with data.accounts array and data.total
view accounts --group=personal --json filters by group
view transactions --json returns envelope with data.transactions array and data.count
view transactions supports --account, --group, --from, --to, --limit filters
Both commands appear in fin tools --json catalog
Text mode uses existing table rendering from output.ts
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Use getAssetAccounts(), getLatestBalances(), getTransactions() from @fin/core. Reference existing packages/cli/src/commands/view.ts for text output patterns

userStories.title: Implement view ledger and view balance commands
userStories.description: As an agent, I need view ledger --json and view balance --json to query journal entries and the balance sheet
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/view/ledger.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'view.ledger'
File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/view/balance.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'view.balance'
view ledger --json returns envelope with data.entries array, data.count, data.total
view ledger supports --account, --from, --to, --limit filters
view balance --json returns envelope with data.assets, data.liabilities, data.equity, data.income, data.expenses, data.netWorth, data.netIncome
view balance --as-of=DATE filters by date
Both commands appear in fin tools --json catalog and are wired in view/index.ts
Text mode uses existing table rendering
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Use getJournalEntries(), getJournalEntryCount(), getBalanceSheet() from @fin/core. Reference existing view.ts for text patterns

userStories.title: Implement report cashflow command
userStories.description: As an agent, I need report cashflow --group=personal --json to get monthly income/expense/net/savings data
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/report/index.ts exporting reportCommand group
File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/report/cashflow.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'report.cashflow'
report cashflow --group=personal --json returns envelope with data.series array and data.totals
Supports --months, --from, --expenses flags
--expenses flag switches to getGroupPureMonthlyCashflowSeries()
--group is required; INVALID_GROUP error if unrecognized
Appears in fin tools --json catalog
Text mode renders existing table output
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Use getGroupMonthlyCashflowSeries(), getGroupPureMonthlyCashflowSeries() from @fin/core. Reference existing report.ts cashflow handler

userStories.title: Implement report health and report runway commands
userStories.description: As an agent, I need report health --json for financial health metrics and report runway --json for months of cash remaining
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/report/health.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'report.health'
File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/report/runway.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'report.runway'
report health --group=personal --json returns envelope with data.series and data.latest
report runway --group=personal --json returns envelope with data.series and data.latest
report runway --consolidated --include=personal,business --json returns consolidated data with data.groups
Both support --from, --to filters and require --group (except consolidated mode)
Both appear in fin tools --json catalog
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Use getGroupDailyHealthSeries(), getGroupDailyRunwaySeries(), getConsolidatedDailyRunwaySeries() from @fin/core. Note: 'report health' is financial health, not system health

userStories.title: Implement report reserves and report categories commands
userStories.description: As an agent, I need report reserves --json for reserve breakdowns and report categories --json for spending by category
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/report/reserves.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'report.reserves'
File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/report/categories.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'report.categories'
report reserves --group=business --json returns envelope with data.series and data.latest
report categories --group=personal --json returns envelope with data.categories and data.total
report categories --mode=median returns data.categories and data.estimatedMonthly
categories supports --months, --limit, --mode=breakdown|median flags
Both appear in fin tools --json catalog
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Use getGroupDailyReserveBreakdownSeries(), getGroupCategoryBreakdown(), getGroupCategoryMonthlyMedian() from @fin/core. categories --mode flattens the old breakdown/median subcommand nesting

userStories.title: Implement report audit command
userStories.description: As an agent, I need report audit --json to get payee breakdown for a specific expense account
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/report/audit.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'report.audit'
report audit --account=Expenses:Business:Uncategorized --json returns envelope with data.payees array and data.total
--account is required; INVALID_INPUT error if missing
Supports --months, --group optional filters
Appears in fin tools --json catalog
Text mode renders payee table
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Use getExpenseAccountPayeeBreakdown() from @fin/core. Reference existing report.ts audit handler

userStories.title: Implement report summary command (text mode)
userStories.description: As a CLI developer, I need report summary to produce the existing markdown financial report, migrated from the legacy summary.ts to the new directory structure
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/report/summary.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'report.summary'
report summary (text mode) produces the same markdown output as the existing summary.ts
Supports --months, --group, --output flags
All helper functions from summary.ts are preserved or inlined
Appears in fin tools --json catalog and is wired in report/index.ts
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Migrate from packages/cli/src/commands/summary.ts (464 lines, 20 helpers). Text mode output is the priority here; JSON mode added in next story

userStories.title: Add JSON structured data mode to report summary
userStories.description: As an agent, I need report summary --json to return structured financial data (groups, consolidated, balanceSheet) instead of markdown
acceptanceCriteria: report summary --json returns envelope with data.generatedAt, data.periodMonths, data.currency, data.groups array, data.consolidated, data.balanceSheet
Each group in data.groups has id, label, balances, snapshot (runway, lastMonthNet, netWorth, medianSpend), assetAllocation, lastMonth, cashflow, incomeSources, expenseSinks, expenseTree
data.consolidated has totalBalance, runway, burnRate
data.balanceSheet has assets, liabilities, equity, income, expenses, netWorth, netIncome
Text mode still produces markdown (ENG-012 behavior preserved)
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Extract the raw data structures that the markdown is built from. Return them directly in JSON mode instead of generating markdown. See .tmp/AGENT_NATIVE.md line 566 for expected JSON shape

userStories.title: Implement import command
userStories.description: As an agent, I need fin import --json to run the import pipeline and get structured results
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/import/index.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'import'

import --json returns envelope with data containing processedFiles, skippedFiles, totalTransactions, duplicateTransactions, journalEntriesCreated, transferPairsCreated, entryErrors, accountsTouched, unmappedDescriptions, archivedFiles
Supports --inbox optional path override
Partial success pattern: ok: true with errors in data when some files fail
Appears in fin tools --json catalog and is wired in main.ts
Text mode preserves existing import output behavior
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Use importInbox() from @fin/core. Reference existing packages/cli/src/commands/import.ts. This is a mutating (non-idempotent) command

userStories.title: Implement sanitize discover, migrate, and recategorize commands
userStories.description: As an agent, I need sanitize --json commands to find unmapped descriptions and apply mapping rules
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/sanitize/index.ts exporting sanitizeCommand group
File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/sanitize/discover.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'sanitize.discover'
File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/sanitize/migrate.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'sanitize.migrate'
File exists at packages/cli/src/commands/sanitize/recategorize.ts using defineToolCommand() with name 'sanitize.recategorize'
discover --json returns envelope with data.descriptions and data.count
discover --unmapped filters to unmapped only
migrate --json returns envelope with data.plan and data.result (or just plan with --dry-run)
recategorize --json returns same pattern as migrate
All three appear in fin tools --json catalog
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Use discoverDescriptions(), discoverUnmappedDescriptions(), planMigration(), executeMigration(), planRecategorize(), executeRecategorize(), loadRules() from @fin/core. Reference existing sanitize.ts

userStories.title: Create contract test harness and fixtures
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a test harness that can spawn fin commands and validate JSON envelope contracts without requiring a real database
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at packages/cli/tests/contract.test.ts with test harness setup
Harness uses EnvelopeCapture + setCaptureMode() for in-process testing (no subprocess spawning needed for most tests)
Helper function validateEnvelope(json) checks: parses as JSON, has ok field, if ok:true has data+meta, if ok:false has error.code+message+hint+meta
Helper function runTool(command, args) invokes a command in capture mode and returns the envelope
At least tools and health commands have passing contract tests
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Reference .tmp/let/packages/cli/tests/contract.test.ts. Use Bun test runner. In-process capture mode avoids subprocess overhead

userStories.title: Add contract tests for all tool commands and parity gate
userStories.description: As a developer, I need contract tests validating JSON envelope output for all 17 registered tools, plus an end-to-end parity gate test
acceptanceCriteria: Contract tests exist for: config.show, config.validate, view.accounts, view.transactions, view.ledger, view.balance, report.cashflow, report.health, report.runway, report.reserves, report.categories, report.audit, report.summary, import, sanitize.discover, sanitize.migrate, sanitize.recategorize
Each test validates: stdout is valid JSON, has ok field, correct meta.tool name
File exists at packages/cli/tests/parity-gate.test.ts with end-to-end agent loop test
Parity gate runs: tools --json -> health --json -> config show --json -> view accounts --json -> view balance --json -> report summary --json
All tests pass
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Commands that need a database may test error envelopes (NO_DATABASE) if no fixture DB is available. The parity gate verifies the complete agent orientation workflow

userStories.title: Remove legacy command files
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the old monolithic command files removed since all functionality has been migrated to the new directory-based structure
acceptanceCriteria: File packages/cli/src/commands/view.ts is deleted
File packages/cli/src/commands/report.ts is deleted
File packages/cli/src/commands/sanitize.ts is deleted
File packages/cli/src/commands/import.ts is deleted
File packages/cli/src/commands/summary.ts is deleted
File packages/cli/src/commands/help.ts is deleted
No remaining imports reference deleted files
main.ts wires only new command groups (tools, health, config, view, report, import, sanitize)
fin tools --json lists exactly 17 tools
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Verify all functionality is covered by new commands before deleting. Run all tests after removal

userStories.title: Add registry drift test
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a test that fails if any command is not registered in the tool registry, preventing drift between commands and the tools catalog
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at packages/cli/tests/registry.test.ts
Test scans packages/cli/src/commands/ for defineToolCommand() usage and verifies each appears in toolRegistry[]
Test verifies toolRegistry has exactly 17 entries
Test verifies no duplicate tool names in registry
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: This prevents adding a new command file that forgets to use defineToolCommand(). Can use glob + grep to scan files or import the registry directly

userStories.title: Create /fin Claude Code skill file
userStories.description: As a Claude Code user, I need a /fin skill that provides autonomous financial analysis via the CLI toolbelt
acceptanceCriteria: File exists at .claude/skills/fin/SKILL.md with frontmatter (name, description, compatibility, allowed-tools)
Skill includes Orientation section (tools, health, config show)
Skill includes Self-describing CLI section referencing fin tools --json
Skill includes Common workflows: quick snapshot, investigate spending, check financial health, import new data
Skill includes Expected output section describing what agent should return
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Reference .tmp/AGENT_NATIVE.md lines 640-704 for the exact skill content. Also reference .tmp/let/.claude/skills/let/SKILL.md for structure

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md with agent-native CLI documentation
userStories.description: As a developer or agent, I need CLAUDE.md to reflect the new tool surface, JSON envelope contracts, and agent workflow
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md section 6 (CLI) updated to show all 17 tools plus 2 infrastructure commands
CLAUDE.md includes JSON envelope contract documentation (success/error shapes, exit codes, error codes)
CLAUDE.md includes agent workflow diagram (orient -> understand -> investigate -> maintain -> report)
CLAUDE.md references /fin skill
Existing sections (Documentation, Repository Structure, Stack, Architecture, Configuration, Quality) preserved
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: Reference .tmp/AGENT_NATIVE.md sections on JSON Envelope Contract and Agent Workflow. Keep CLAUDE.md concise -- link to AGENT_NATIVE.md for exhaustive details

userStories.title: Final validation of complete agent-native CLI
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify the entire agent-native CLI works end-to-end with all quality gates passing
acceptanceCriteria: bun run util:check passes (format + lint + types + tests)
bun run fin tools --json lists exactly 17 tools
bun run fin health --json returns ready or degraded status (not blocked)
bun test packages/cli/tests/contract.test.ts passes
bun test packages/cli/tests/parity-gate.test.ts passes
bun test packages/cli/tests/registry.test.ts passes
No legacy command files remain in packages/cli/src/commands/
Quality gate passes
userStories.notes: This is the final validation story. Fix any remaining issues discovered during full integration testing
